decision.reason_detail: 'cause' here refers to an external legal/social ground for divorce, not an inner-being characteristic or condition

verses.verse_text: Mat 20:18 “ See , we are going up to Jerusalem . And the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes , and they will condemn him to death

decision.reason_detail: The term refers to an external judicial act — the chief priests and scribes pronouncing a death sentence — not an inner-being characteristic.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: they will condemn him to death

verses.verse_text: Mat 27:3 Then when Judas , his betrayer , saw that Jesus was condemned , he changed his mind and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders ,

decision.brief_summary: Judas recognises Jesus has been condemned, prompting inner moral remorse and a change of mind.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: saw that Jesus was condemned, he changed his mind

verses.verse_text: Mat 27:37 And over his head they put the charge against him , which read , “ This is Jesus , the King of the Jews .”

decision.reason_detail: The term names the written legal charge posted on the cross, an external juridical/civic label, not an inner-being condition.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: they put the charge against him

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:33 Yet you have been righteous in all that has come upon us, for you have dealt faithfully and we have acted wickedly .

decision.brief_summary: God is declared righteous as an inner-being characteristic contrasted with human wickedness.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: you have been righteous in all that has come upon us

verses.verse_text: Num 23:4 and God met Balaam . And Balaam said to him, “I have arranged the seven altars and I have offered on each altar a bull and a ram .”

decision.reason_detail: The term here refers to arranging/setting altars for ritual sacrifice — an external cultic action, not an inner-being quality or moral assessment.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: I have arranged the seven altars

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:24 One gives freely, yet grows all the richer ; another withholds what he should give , and only suffers want .

decision.reason_detail: The term underlies what one 'should give,' describing an external standard of proper conduct or due amount rather than an inner-being quality of uprightness held within the person.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: withholds what he should give

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:31 Do not look at wine when it is red , when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly .

decision.reason_detail: The term here describes wine going down smoothly, a physical sensation with no inner-being or moral uprightness content.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: goes down smoothly

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:2 She has slaughtered her beasts ; she has mixed her wine ; she has also set her table .

decision.reason_detail: The term here refers to setting/arranging a table, a physical domestic action with no inner-being content.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: she has also set her table

verses.verse_text: Psa 116:5 Gracious is the Lord , and righteous ; our God is merciful .

decision.brief_summary: God is declared righteous as an inner characteristic alongside graciousness and mercy.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: Gracious is the Lord, and righteous

verses.verse_text: Tsadhe Psa 119:137 Righteous are you , O Lord , and right are your rules .

decision.brief_summary: God is declared righteous as an inner-being characteristic, paired with the righteousness of His rules.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: Righteous are you, O Lord

verses.verse_text: Psa 129:4 The Lord is righteous ; he has cut the cords of the wicked .

decision.brief_summary: The Lord is described as righteous, presenting righteousness as a divine inner-being characteristic motivating just action.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: The Lord is righteous

verses.verse_text: Psa 132:17 There I will make a horn to sprout for David ; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed .

decision.reason_detail: The term 'to value/prepare' here refers to an external act of preparation (a lamp for the anointed), not an inner-being characteristic of righteousness or justice.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: I have prepared a lamp for my anointed

verses.verse_text: Psa 145:17 The Lord is righteous in all his ways and kind in all his works .

decision.brief_summary: The Lord is declared righteous in all his ways, naming righteousness as an inner characteristic of God.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: The Lord is righteous in all his ways

verses.verse_text: Psa 40:9 I have told the glad news of deliverance in the great congregation ; behold , I have not restrained my lips , as you know , O Lord .

decision.brief_summary: The psalmist proclaims righteousness/deliverance openly in the congregation, expressing an inner conviction to declare it.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: told the glad news of deliverance in the great congregation

verses.verse_text: Psa 45:4 In your majesty ride out victoriously for the cause of truth and meekness and righteousness ; let your right hand teach you awesome deeds !

decision.brief_summary: Righteousness is named as an inner quality or cause for which the king rides out victoriously.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: for the cause of truth and meekness and righteousness

verses.verse_text: Psa 50:21 These things you have done , and I have been silent ; you thought that I was one like yourself. But now I rebuke you and lay the charge before you.

decision.reason_detail: The term 'to value/lay the charge' functions here as an external judicial act of laying charges before someone, not an inner-being characteristic.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: lay the charge before you

verses.verse_text: Psa 78:19 They spoke against God , saying , “ Can God spread a table in the wilderness ?

decision.reason_detail: The term 'to value' here relates to setting/spreading a table, a physical/external action with no inner-being righteousness or justice content.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: spread a table in the wilderness

verses.verse_text: Psa 7:11 God is a righteous judge , and a God who feels indignation every day .

decision.brief_summary: God is described as a righteous judge, naming righteousness as an inner characteristic of His judicial nature.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: God is a righteous judge

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:36 As it is written , “For your sake we are being killed all the day long ; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered .”
verses.short_def_mounce: to credit, count, reckon; regard, think, consider

decision.reason_detail: The term here means 'regarded as' in the sense of being treated/classified as slaughter-sheep — an external social/violent condition, not an inner-being quality.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:8 This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God , but the children of the promise are counted as offspring .
verses.short_def_mounce: to credit, count, reckon; regard, think, consider

decision.reason_detail: 'counted as offspring' is a declarative/accounting reckoning of lineage status, not an inner-being characteristic of righteousness or justice.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: children of the promise are counted as offspring

verses.verse_text: Song 7:9 and your mouth like the best wine . It goes down smoothly for my beloved , gliding over lips and teeth .

decision.reason_detail: The term here describes wine flowing smoothly, a physical/sensory quality, not an inner-being moral characteristic.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: goes down smoothly for my beloved, gliding over lips

verses.verse_text: Tit 1:13 This testimony is true . Therefore rebuke them sharply , that they may be sound in the faith ,

decision.reason_detail: The verse concerns rebuking for soundness in faith; the term 'cause/charge' does not appear in the verse text in a way that engages an inner-being characteristic.

verses.verse_text: Zep 3:5 The Lord within her is righteous ; he does no injustice ; every morning he shows forth his justice ; each dawn he does not fail ; but the unjust knows no shame .

decision.brief_summary: The Lord dwelling within is characterised as righteous, an inner quality contrasted with the unjust who knows no shame.
decision.verse_evidence_quote: The Lord within her is righteous; he does no injustice
